name: Western Ontario and McMaster Universities Osteoarthritis Index (WOMAC)
purpose: Assess pain, stiffness, and physical function in patients with hip and/or knee osteoarthritis.
score_range: 0 to 96 (with subscales: Pain 0-20, Stiffness 0-8, Physical Function 0-68).
interpretation: Higher scores indicate worse pain, stiffness, and functional limitations.

name: Disabilities of the Arm, Shoulder, and Hand (DASH)
purpose: Measure physical function and symptoms in patients with any musculoskeletal disorder of the upper limb.
interpretation: A score of 0 indicates no disability, while a score of 100 indicates the most severe disability or full functional limitation.

name: Oswestry Disability Index (ODI)
purpose: Assess the level of functional disability and evaluate impact of low back pain on activities of daily living.
score_range: 0% to 100% (or 0 to 50 raw points, converted to percentage)
interpretation: 0-20%: Minimal disability, 21-40%: Moderate disability, 41-60%: Severe disability, 61-80%: Crippling, 81-100%: Bed-bound or exaggerating symptoms.

name: Berg Balance Scale (BBS)
purpose: Assess static and dynamic balance, and evaluate fall risk in older adults or neurological populations.
interpretation: 41-56: Low fall risk, 21-40: Medium fall risk (requires assistive device), 0-20: High fall risk (wheelchair bound or high hazard).

name: Functional Independence Measure (FIM)
purpose: Measure the level of safety, independence, and assistance required by an individual to perform activities of daily living (ADLs).
interpretation: Higher scores indicate greater independent functioning. A score of 18 indicates total assistance (complete dependence) across all items, while 126 indicates complete independence.

name: American Spinal Injury Association (ASIA) Impairment Scale
purpose: Standardize the classification of neurological levels and the severity of spinal cord injuries.
interpretation: Grade A: Complete motor and sensory loss; Grade B: Incomplete sensory preservation only; Grade C: Incomplete motor preservation (more than half of key muscles below neurological level are Grade < 3); Grade D: Incomplete motor preservation (half or more key muscles are Grade >= 3); Grade E: Normal sensory and motor function.

name: Unified Parkinson's Disease Rating Scale (MDS-UPDRS)
purpose: Evaluate the course, progression, and burden of Parkinson's Disease across cognitive, motor, and daily living areas.
interpretation: Subdivided into four parts (Part I: Non-motor experiences of daily living, Part II: Motor experiences of daily living, Part III: Motor examination, Part IV: Motor complications). Higher scores indicate more severe disease severity.

name: Gross Motor Function Measure (GMFM)
purpose: Evaluate change over time in gross motor function of children with cerebral palsy and other motor disabilities.
score_range: 0% to 100% across 5 domains (Lying & Rolling, Sitting, Crawling & Kneeling, Standing, Walking/Running/Jumping)
interpretation: Higher percentages indicate greater gross motor capability. Calculated relative to age expectations.

name: Modified Ashworth Scale (MAS)
purpose: Measure the velocity-dependent resistance or tone in spastic muscles.
interpretation: 0: No increase in muscle tone; 1: Slight increase in tone, giving catch-and-release or minimal resistance at end-range; 1+: Slight increase in tone, manifested by a catch followed by minimal resistance throughout the remainder (less than half) of the ROM; 2: Marked increase in tone through most of the ROM, but affected part moved easily; 3: Considerable increase in tone, passive movement difficult; 4: Affected part rigid in flexion or extension.

name: Numeric Pain Rating Scale (NPRS)
purpose: Provide an objective, standardized numeric measurement of a patient's pain intensity.
interpretation: 0: No pain; 1-3: Mild pain; 4-6: Moderate pain; 7-10: Severe pain.
